GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "How to get glowing skin, according to the experts", "A flawless complexion is more than skin deep.", "Vogue asks Vida Glow's four skin panellists for their expert tips when it comes to achieving glowing skin. "When clients come to me, I start by asking about their lifestyle. Skincare is more holistic than just putting things on the skin. So, a healthy, low GI diet is key. Ingestible marine collagen supplements also have robust science to support them in terms of skin hydration and reducing fine lines and wrinkles. Hydrolysed marine collagen is superior to other collagens because the component parts, peptides and amino acids, are made so small that they cross through the gut and into the bloodstream," says scientists Dr Michele Squire. "There's a problematic obsession with actives in skincare right now. Many of my clients are focused not only on incorporating them into their routines, but at maximum levels. We've forgotten about the basics. A solid cleanser and moisturiser are key to keeping the skin healthy. Your skin can only tolerate so much," adds Squire. "Supplements can be an important part of a healthy diet, especially ones that focus on optimal skin and nail health... Marine collagen is another supplement that helps skin. It stimulates fibroblast cells to produce elastin within the dermis layer of the skin, thickening and plumping the surface of the skin from within," says beauty therapist Jocelyn Petroni. "It's something I take every morning when I'm breastfeeding Georgia – it's easily added to a glass of water. The science behind Vida Glow is so impressive that I quickly see visible results in the skin and nails of my clients," adds Petroni. It's not new advice, but hydration is one of dietician Chloe McLeod's top tips for glowing skin. "It's absolutely key," she says. While standard recommendations suggest two litres per day, McLeod says, "It's important to look at the climate you're living in. If you're somewhere hot and humid, you're going to need more water. Sitting in air conditioning all day or working out a lot? Same thing." "In the past, people got a lot more collagen from their food, through the boiling of bones. Given that we don't do nose-to-tail dining as much today, our consumption of collagen is a lot lower. Just like we might supplement with iron for an iron deficiency, including collagen in your diet in this way is a safe, tolerable approach towards improving the health and appearance of skin," she says, adding that it's not just about beauty. Collagen also helps to improve the joint health, assist in reducing sugar cravings and boost muscle repair. So what does everyone need for healthy skin? "Moisturiser. It has a host of benefits in terms of keeping your skin supple, clean and bright. I think it's an essential cornerstone of achieving healthy skin for everybody. No matter your skin type, you need one. By moisturising, you're less likely to get cracks in between the skin cells, so it's effectively ensuring your barrier remains intact," says dermatologist Dr Philip Tong. "For patients that already practise proper skin health, Vida Glow can deliver that next level of beautiful skin. I'd recommend it to patients with dry or blemished skin, as marine collagen is said to help rejuvenate and have anti-inflammatory properties. There are still clinical trials being done, but it's exciting that something as simple as ingested collagen can improve your overall complexion," explains Tong."
